sections.paragraph:
διαπαρατριβή, ῆς, ἡ (heightened form of παρατριβή ‘irritation, friction’ Polyb. 2, 36, 5 et al.; B-D-F §116, 4) that which is characterized by constant argumentativeness and therefore irritating, wrangling pl. frictional wranglings (AMalherbe, Paul and the Popular Philosophers ’89, 125, n. 9) 1 Ti 6:5 (v.l. παραδιατριβαί).—DELG s.v. τρίβω.

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

  1. pl. = plural

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 235
